fact: July 15 is exactly 196 days into the year - that's more than halfway through! If the year were a pizza, you'd have already eaten 6 out of 10 slices.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat time when many deserts reach their most scorching temperatures - some places hit 120°F or hotter!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this date in North America! These bug musicians can make sounds up to 120 decibels - that's as loud as a chainsaw.
Fireflies often blink most actively in mid-July during their peak mating season, flashing special light codes to find mates in the warm summer nights.
July 15 falls in the middle of Cancer season in the zodiac - people born around now are supposedly extra moody and love staying home, just like crabs hiding in their shells.
In Antarctica, this date marks the dead of winter when temperatures can drop to -80°F and the sun hasn't been seen for months - it's like living inside a freezer in total darkness!
Summer flowers are growing rapidly right now! Sunflowers can grow up to 2 inches per day in mid-July heat, racing to become giant yellow towers.
The sun typically sets latest around this time in many northern places - in some cities, daylight lasts until almost 10 PM, giving kids extra time to play outside.
Mosquitoes are usually at their most annoying peak right now because hot, humid July weather creates perfect breeding conditions - they can go from egg to biting pest in just one week!
Ocean water often reaches its most perfect swimming temperature around mid-July in many coastal areas, warming up to a toasty 75-80°F after months of soaking up summer sun.
Bears in North America are typically getting super chubby right now! July is prime berry season, so bears stuff themselves with up to 20,000 calories per day - that's like eating 40 hamburgers!
Thunder and lightning storms are often most explosive around this date because hot air and cool air crash into each other more dramatically, creating those awesome booming summer storms.
